content.full_text:
October 22, 1990

VIA FACSIMILE TRANSMISSION

Peggy Bernardi, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Advice Letter No. 90-643

Dear Ms. Bernardi:

This letter relates to my request for advice on October 4, 1990. As I stated in my letter, Councilmember Hammond's participation in a vote of the Irvine City Council is legally required because the City Council currently has only four, as opposed to the normal five, members. The fifth seat was the subject of litigation pending in the Court of Appeal, and you had requested that I inform you immediately when the court ruled in the case.

The court ruled yesterday not to fill the vacant seat, and ordered an election to fill the seat on November 6. (See enclosed copy of article from 10/19/90 Orange County Register.) Thus, the City Council still has only four members and will have only four members for its October 23 meeting on the emergency ordinance.

Since the Irvine City Charter mandates that emergency ordinances require four votes of the City Council to be extended or amended, Councilmember Hammond's participation in the decision on the ordinance is "legally required," as that term is defined in Government Code section 87101 and 2 California Code of Regulations section 18701.

The City Council decision is less than a week away. I had asked in my letter that the FPPC respond before that time. Will the FPPC have a response ready in time for Councilmember Hammond to participate? If not, please advise me immediately.

Peggy Bernardi, Esq.
October 22, 1990
Page 2

Thank you again for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton

JRS/sfr
Enclosure
6560.01
